rulings.Case Title: BENITO CARRERA et al VS. TRACTEL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint Or In The Alternative, For Summary Ajudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT ARTERRA OWNERS ASSOCIATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Ajudication. Denied. Privette and its progeny do not require granting of this motion. Arterra did not satisfy its burden of production. "A landowner cannot effectively delegate to the contractor responsibility for the safety of its employees if it fails to disclose critical information needed to fulfill that responsibility, and therefore the landowner would be liable to the contractor's employee if the employee's injury is attributable to an undisclosed hazard. Nothing in the Privette line of cases suggests the contrary. As in Hooker and McKown, the hirer's liability in such circumstances would be derived from the hirer's rather than the contractor's negligence." (Kinsman v. Unocal Corp. (2005) 37 Cal.4th 659, 674.) In Koephich v. Kashiwa Fudosan America, Inc. (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 32, 36-37, the court explains that: " 'The duty which a possessor of land owes to others to put and maintain it in reasonably safe condition is nondelegable. If an independent contractor, no matter how carefully selected, is employed to perform it, the possessor is answerable for harm caused by the negligent failure of his contractor to put or maintain the buildings and structures in reasonably safe condition, irrespective of whether the contractor's negligence lies in his incompetence, carelessness, inattention or delay.' " The undisputed evidence demonstrates that Arterra owned the defective pedestal that caused plaintiffs' injuries. There is a triable issue whether Arterra knew or should have known of the undisclosed hazard. (Lum Depo., 130:13-21; Anderson Dec., par. 9). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing Defendant The Regents Of The University Of Californias Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant Supplemental Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 14, Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California's Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories; Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant's Supplemental Discovery Requests. Granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to Special Interrogatories nos. 6-8. Plaintiff shall respond within five days of entry of this Order. Any information produced by Plaintiff in response to Special Interrogatories nos. 6-8 shall be marked "Confidential" and shall only be viewed by the attorneys and the Court. Further, the amount and/or terms of the settlement agreement may only be used for purposes of calculating an offset against any jury award. Granted as to the Supplemental Interrogatories and Supplemental Requests for Production. Plaintiff shall respond to these supplemental discovery requests within five days of entry of this Order. The Request for Production, Set Two, is denied as moot because of Plaintiff's counsel's representation that Plaintiff waives any claim for loss of past or future earnings. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: JANET KASSOUF VS. RICHARD WEAVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel For Gophotobooks.Com
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JANET KASSOUF'S Motion To Disqualify Counsel For Gophotobooks.Com. Denied. Plaintiff has standing to bring this motion. Blue Water Sunset, LLC v. Markowitz (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 477 and Forrest v. Baeza (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 65, hold that majority or equal shareholders accused of wrongdoing cannot unilaterally waive, on behalf of the corporation, a conflict arising from an attorney's representation of the corporation and the accused shareholders. Otherwise, the corporation would be "defenseless when it is most vulnerable, i.e., when it is represented by an attorney who has a conflict because he also represents and is beholden to a company insider who injured the company." (Blue Water Sunset, LLC v. Markowitz (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 477, 486.) Although counsel for GOPhotobooks.com maintains there is no conflict, and therefore waiver is not at issue here, the policy behind the reasoning in Blue Water Sunset and Forrest applies. If the right to disqualify lay solely with the accused shareholders, the corporation would be defenseless to any impermissible conflict. However, there is no basis for disqualification here. Mr. Kirsch's previous representation of the Blazicks is not a ground for disqualification because there is no "substantial relationship" between the subjects of the former and current representation. (Forrest v. Baeza (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 65, 73.) Plaintiff has not established that any disclosures she made at the meeting with Mr. Kirsch in March 2013 are "substantially related" to the current representation. (Elliott v. McFarland Unified School Dist. (1985) 165 Cal.App.3d 562, 568.) Plaintiff argues that the information regarding the promissory note is substantially related to Mr. Kirsch's current representation of GOPhotobooks.com because the Blazicks seek information regarding the promissory note in connection with their cross-complaint. GoPhotobooks.com is not a party to the cross-complaint. Plaintiff fails to show how confidential information disclosed to Mr. Kirsch could be used by the Blazicks, who are represented by different counsel. Lastly, paragraph 4 of the Declaration of Joshua Kirsch establishes that his appointment as counsel for GOPhotobooks.com was not an ultra vires act. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN LEGARIE VS. MAHMOUD ASSAF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1(B) And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT TALAB QARAJAH's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1(B) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted as to the following: FI 17.1 as to RFA 1, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 16, 17, 31, 32, 33. The Motion is denied as to the following: FI 17.1 as to RFAs 11 ("holder of record is ambiguous"), 15 ("registered as a shareholder" is ambiguous, 20, 24, 25, 26, 29 (plaintiff answered these interrogatories). The fact that responding party copied factual allegations from his verified complaint does not make the responses to form interrogatory non-responsive. However, references to, for example, "paragraphs 7 through 68" to the Verified First Amended Complaint are not code-compliant responses. Plaintiff must make a good faith to provide clear and straightforward responses to each interrogatory and not simply repeat responses to interrogatories. Moving party's request for sanctions is denied per CCP 2023.040. Responding party's request for sanctions is denied under CCP 2023.040(a) because the Court does not find that moving party engaged in a misuse of the discovery process. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS TIE LIU, T J CONSTRUCTION INC., AND SURETEC INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD ONG ET AL VS. TIE JUN LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS TIE LIU, T J CONSTRUCTION INC., AND SURETEC INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to all causes of action and the motion to strike is taken off calendar as moot. All claims are inadequately pled. While brevity is generally a good idea in pleadings, where, as here, brevity is at the cost of giving adequate notice to defendants of the basic facts that they need to defend against, brevity needs to give away to the demands of notice pleading. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MAGTOGO, LLC VS. CERTAIN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 10, CROSS-DEFENDANT MAGTOGO, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. Overruled with twenty days to answer. On a demurrer, a court is required to accept the explicit or implicit interpretation of disputed contract language that is advanced by the challenged pleading, as long as that interpretation is plausible, which it is here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY JOHNS ET AL VS. JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Jones Memorial Homes Inc., Gerald Jones, Emmett Cobb Jr., Charles Harris, Rozelle Lee, James Mccray Jr., Derek Tolliver, And Marvin Norman
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 3, 2015, Line 8, PLAINTIFFS JOHNNY JOHNS AND PAMELA KELLY'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Jones Memorial Homes Inc., Gerald Jones, Emmett Cobb Jr., Charles Harris, Rozelle Lee, James Mccray Jr., Derek Tolliver, And Marvin Norman Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is continued to June 17, 2015 at 9:00 AM. All parties are ordered to deliver courtesy copies of all moving, opposing, and reply papers to Department 302 by June 9, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
